# h3

```go
import "github.com/uber/h3-go/v4"
```

Package h3 is the go binding for Uber's H3 Geo Index system. It uses cgo to link with a statically compiled h3 library

## Constants

<a name="MaxCellBndryVerts"></a>

```go
const (
    // MaxCellBndryVerts is the maximum number of vertices that can be used
    // to represent the shape of a cell.
    MaxCellBndryVerts = C.MAX_CELL_BNDRY_VERTS

    // MaxResolution is the maximum H3 resolution a LatLng can be indexed to.
    MaxResolution = C.MAX_H3_RES

    // NumIcosaFaces is the number of faces on an icosahedron.
    NumIcosaFaces = C.NUM_ICOSA_FACES

    // NumBaseCells is the number of H3 base cells.
    NumBaseCells = C.NUM_BASE_CELLS

    // NumPentagons is the number of H3 pentagon cells (same at every resolution).
    NumPentagons = C.NUM_PENTAGONS

    // InvalidH3Index is a sentinel value for an invalid H3 index.
    InvalidH3Index = C.H3_NULL

    // DegsToRads converts degrees to radians by multiplying degrees by this constant.
    DegsToRads = math.Pi / 180.0
    // RadsToDegs converts radians to degrees by multiplying radians by this constant.
    RadsToDegs = 180.0 / math.Pi
)
```

## Variables

<a name="ErrFailed"></a>Error codes.

```go
var (
    ErrFailed                = errors.New("the operation failed")
    ErrDomain                = errors.New("argument was outside of acceptable range")
    ErrLatLngDomain          = errors.New("latitude or longitude arguments were outside of acceptable range")
    ErrResolutionDomain      = errors.New("resolution argument was outside of acceptable range")
    ErrCellInvalid           = errors.New("H3Index cell argument was not valid")
    ErrDirectedEdgeInvalid   = errors.New("H3Index directed edge argument was not valid")
    ErrUndirectedEdgeInvalid = errors.New("H3Index undirected edge argument was not valid")
    ErrVertexInvalid         = errors.New("H3Index vertex argument was not valid")
    ErrPentagon              = errors.New("pentagon distortion was encountered")
    ErrDuplicateInput        = errors.New("duplicate input was encountered in the arguments")
    ErrNotNeighbors          = errors.New("H3Index cell arguments were not neighbors")
    ErrRsolutionMismatch     = errors.New("H3Index cell arguments had incompatible resolutions")
    ErrMemoryAlloc           = errors.New("necessary memory allocation failed")
    ErrMemoryBounds          = errors.New("bounds of provided memory were not large enough")
    ErrOptionInvalid         = errors.New("mode or flags argument was not valid")
    ErrIndexInvalid          = errors.New("index argument was not valid")
    ErrBaseCellDomain        = errors.New("base cell number was outside of acceptable range")
    ErrDigitDomain           = errors.New("child digits invalid")
    ErrDeletedDigit          = errors.New("deleted subsequence indicates invalid index")
)
```

<a name="GridDiskDistances"></a>

## func [GridDiskDistances](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L306>)

```go
func GridDiskDistances(origin Cell, k int) ([][]Cell, error)
```

GridDiskDistances produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell. This method optimistically tries the faster GridDiskDistancesUnsafe first. If a cell was a pentagon or was in the pentagon distortion area, it falls back to GridDiskDistancesSafe.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Outer slice is ordered from origin outwards. Inner slices are in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="GridDiskDistancesSafe"></a>

## func [GridDiskDistancesSafe](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L396>)

```go
func GridDiskDistancesSafe(origin Cell, k int) ([][]Cell, error)
```

GridDiskDistancesSafe produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell. This is the safe, but slow version of GridDiskDistances.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Outer slice is ordered from origin outwards. Inner slices are in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="GridDiskDistancesUnsafe"></a>

## func [GridDiskDistancesUnsafe](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L352>)

```go
func GridDiskDistancesUnsafe(origin Cell, k int) ([][]Cell, error)
```

GridDiskDistancesUnsafe produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell. Output behavior is undefined when one of the cells returned by this function is a pentagon or is in the pentagon distortion area.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Outer slice is ordered from origin outwards. Inner slices are in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="GridDisksUnsafe"></a>

## func [GridDisksUnsafe](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L281>)

```go
func GridDisksUnsafe(origins []Cell, k int) ([][]Cell, error)
```

GridDisksUnsafe produces cells within grid distance k of all provided origin cells.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Outer slice is ordered in the same order origins were passed in. Inner slices are in no particular order.

This does not call through to the underlying C.gridDisksUnsafe implementation as it is slightly easier to do so to avoid unnecessary type conversions.

<a name="GridDistance"></a>

## func [GridDistance](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1078>)

```go
func GridDistance(a, b Cell) (int, error)
```

GridDistance returns grid distance between two cells.

This function may fail to find the distance between two indexes, for example if they are very far apart. It may also fail when finding distances for indexes on opposite sides of a pentagon.

```go

```

<a name="Cell"></a>

## type [Cell](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L142>)

Cell is an Index that identifies a single hexagon cell at a resolution.

```go
type Cell int64
```

<a name="CellFromString"></a>

### func [CellFromString](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L796>)

```go
func CellFromString(s string) Cell
```

CellFromString returns a Cell from a string. Should call c.IsValid\(\) to check if the Cell is valid before using it.

<a name="ChildPosToCell"></a>

### func [ChildPosToCell](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1044>)

```go
func ChildPosToCell(position int, a Cell, resolution int) (Cell, error)
```

ChildPosToCell returns the child of cell a at a given position within an ordered list of all children at the specified resolution.

<a name="CompactCells"></a>

### func [CompactCells](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1013>)

```go
func CompactCells(in []Cell) ([]Cell, error)
```

CompactCells merges full sets of children into their parent H3Index recursively, until no more merges are possible.

<a name="GridDisk"></a>

### func [GridDisk](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L252>)

```go
func GridDisk(origin Cell, k int) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridDisk produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Output is placed in an array in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="GridPath"></a>

### func [GridPath](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1097>)

```go
func GridPath(a, b Cell) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridPath returns the line of cells between the two cells \(inclusive\).

This function may fail to find the line between two indexes, for example if they are very far apart. It may also fail when finding distances for indexes on opposite sides of a pentagon.

<a name="GridRing"></a>

### func [GridRing](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L435>)

```go
func GridRing(origin Cell, k int) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridRing produces the "hollow" ring of cells at exactly grid distance k from the origin cell.

k\-ring 0 returns just the origin hexagon.

Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="GridRingUnsafe"></a>

### func [GridRingUnsafe](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L456>)

```go
func GridRingUnsafe(origin Cell, k int) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridRingUnsafe produces the "hollow" ring of cells at exactly grid distance k from the origin cell.

k\-ring 0 returns just the origin hexagon.

<a name="LatLngToCell"></a>

### func [LatLngToCell](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L204>)

```go
func LatLngToCell(latLng LatLng, resolution int) (Cell, error)
```

LatLngToCell returns the Cell at resolution for a geographic coordinate.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "fmt"

 "github.com/uber/h3-go/v4"
)

func main() {
 latLng := h3.NewLatLng(37.775938728915946, -122.41795063018799)
 resolution := 9

 c, err := h3.LatLngToCell(latLng, resolution)
 if err != nil {
  panic(err)
 }

 fmt.Printf("%s", c)
}
```

#### Output

```
8928308280fffff
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="LocalIJToCell"></a>

### func [LocalIJToCell](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1138>)

```go
func LocalIJToCell(origin Cell, ij CoordIJ) (Cell, error)
```

LocalIJToCell produces a cell for ij coordinates anchored by an origin.

The coordinate space used by this function may have deleted regions or warping due to pentagonal distortion.

Failure may occur if the index is too far away from the origin or if the index is on the other side of a pentagon.

<a name="Pentagons"></a>

### func [Pentagons](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L744>)

```go
func Pentagons(resolution int) ([]Cell, error)
```

Pentagons returns all the pentagons at resolution.

<a name="PolygonToCells"></a>

### func [PolygonToCells](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L479>)

```go
func PolygonToCells(polygon GeoPolygon, resolution int) ([]Cell, error)
```

PolygonToCells takes a given GeoJSON\-like data structure fills it with the hexagon cells that are contained by the GeoJSON\-like data structure.

This implementation traces the GeoJSON geoloop\(s\) in cartesian space with hexagons, tests them and their neighbors to be contained by the geoloop\(s\), and then any newly found hexagons are used to test again until no new hexagons are found.

<a name="PolygonToCellsExperimental"></a>

### func [PolygonToCellsExperimental](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L505>)

```go
func PolygonToCellsExperimental(polygon GeoPolygon, resolution int, mode ContainmentMode, maxNumCellsReturn ...int64) ([]Cell, error)
```

PolygonToCellsExperimental takes a given GeoJSON\-like data structure fills it with the hexagon cells that are contained by the GeoJSON\-like data structure.

This implementation traces the GeoJSON geoloop\(s\) in cartesian space with hexagons, tests them and their neighbors to be contained by the geoloop\(s\), and then any newly found hexagons are used to test again until no new hexagons are found.

<a name="Res0Cells"></a>

### func [Res0Cells](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L736>)

```go
func Res0Cells() ([]Cell, error)
```

Res0Cells returns all the cells at resolution 0.

<a name="UncompactCells"></a>

### func [UncompactCells](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1026>)

```go
func UncompactCells(in []Cell, resolution int) ([]Cell, error)
```

UncompactCells splits every H3Index in in if its resolution is greater than resolution recursively. Returns all the H3Indexes at resolution resolution.

```go
func (c Cell) BaseCellNumber() int
```

BaseCellNumber returns the integer ID \(0\-121\) of the base cell the H3Index h belongs to.

```go
func (c Cell) Boundary() (CellBoundary, error)
```

Boundary returns a CellBoundary of the Cell.

```go
func (c Cell) CenterChild(resolution int) (Cell, error)
```

CenterChild returns the center child Cell of this Cell.

### func \(Cell\) [ChildPos](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1070>)

```go
func (c Cell) ChildPos(resolution int) (int, error)
```

ChildPos returns the position of the cell within an ordered list of all children of the cell's parent at the specified resolution.

```go
func (c Cell) ChildPosToCell(position int, resolution int) (Cell, error)
```

ChildPosToCell returns the child cell at a given position within an ordered list of all children at the specified resolution.

<a name="Cell.Children"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [Children](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L856>)

```go
func (c Cell) Children(resolution int) ([]Cell, error)
```

Children returns the children or grandchildren cells of this Cell.

<a name="Cell.DirectedEdge"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [DirectedEdge](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L924>)

```go
func (c Cell) DirectedEdge(other Cell) (DirectedEdge, error)
```

DirectedEdge returns a DirectedEdge from this Cell to other.

<a name="Cell.DirectedEdges"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [DirectedEdges](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L932>)

```go
func (c Cell) DirectedEdges() ([]DirectedEdge, error)
```

DirectedEdges returns 6 directed edges with h as the origin.

<a name="Cell.GridDisk"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridDisk](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L266>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridDisk(k int) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridDisk produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Output is placed in an array in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="Cell.GridDiskDistances"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridDiskDistances](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L338>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridDiskDistances(k int) ([][]Cell, error)
```

GridDiskDistances produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell. This method optimistically tries the faster GridDiskDistancesUnsafe first. If a cell was a pentagon or was in the pentagon distortion area, it falls back to GridDiskDistancesSafe.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Outer slice is ordered from origin outwards. Inner slices are in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="Cell.GridDiskDistancesSafe"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridDiskDistancesSafe](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L426>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridDiskDistancesSafe(k int) ([][]Cell, error)
```

GridDiskDistancesSafe produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell. This is the safe, but slow version of GridDiskDistances.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Outer slice is ordered from origin outwards. Inner slices are in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="Cell.GridDiskDistancesUnsafe"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridDiskDistancesUnsafe](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L383>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridDiskDistancesUnsafe(k int) ([][]Cell, error)
```

GridDiskDistancesUnsafe produces cells within grid distance k of the origin cell. Output behavior is undefined when one of the cells returned by this function is a pentagon or is in the pentagon distortion area.

k\-ring 0 is defined as the origin cell, k\-ring 1 is defined as k\-ring 0 and all neighboring cells, and so on.

Outer slice is ordered from origin outwards. Inner slices are in no particular order. Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="Cell.GridDistance"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridDistance](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1089>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridDistance(other Cell) (int, error)
```

GridDistance returns grid distance between two cells.

This function may fail to find the distance between two indexes, for example if they are very far apart. It may also fail when finding distances for indexes on opposite sides of a pentagon.

<a name="Cell.GridPath"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridPath](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1115>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridPath(other Cell) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridPath returns the line of cells between the two cells \(inclusive\).

This function may fail to find the line between two indexes, for example if they are very far apart. It may also fail when finding distances for indexes on opposite sides of a pentagon.

<a name="Cell.GridRing"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridRing](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L449>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridRing(k int) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridRing produces the "hollow" ring of cells at exactly grid distance k from the origin cell.

k\-ring 0 returns just the origin hexagon.

Elements of the output array may be left zero, as can happen when crossing a pentagon.

<a name="Cell.GridRingUnsafe"></a>

### func \(Cell\) [GridRingUnsafe](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L468>)

```go
func (c Cell) GridRingUnsafe(k int) ([]Cell, error)
```

GridRingUnsafe produces the "hollow" ring of cells at exactly grid distance k from the origin cell.

k\-ring 0 returns just the origin hexagon.

<a name="CellBoundary"></a>

## type [CellBoundary](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L168>)

CellBoundary is a slice of LatLng. Note, len\(CellBoundary\) will never exceed MaxCellBndryVerts.

```go
type CellBoundary []LatLng
```

<a name="CellToBoundary"></a>

### func [CellToBoundary](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L232>)

```go
func CellToBoundary(c Cell) (CellBoundary, error)
```

CellToBoundary returns a CellBoundary of the Cell.

<a name="ContainmentMode"></a>

## type [ContainmentMode](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L185>)

ContainmentMode is an int for specifying PolygonToCell containment behavior.

```go
type ContainmentMode C.uint32_t
```

<a name="ContainmentCenter"></a>PolygonToCells containment modes

```go
const (
    ContainmentCenter          ContainmentMode = C.CONTAINMENT_CENTER           // Cell center is contained in the shape
    ContainmentFull            ContainmentMode = C.CONTAINMENT_FULL             // Cell is fully contained in the shape
    ContainmentOverlapping     ContainmentMode = C.CONTAINMENT_OVERLAPPING      // Cell overlaps the shape at any point
    ContainmentOverlappingBbox ContainmentMode = C.CONTAINMENT_OVERLAPPING_BBOX // Cell bounding box overlaps shape
    ContainmentInvalid         ContainmentMode = C.CONTAINMENT_INVALID          // This mode is invalid and should not be used
)
```

<a name="CoordIJ"></a>

## type [CoordIJ](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L162-L164>)

CoordIJ IJ hexagon coordinates

Each axis is spaced 120 degrees apart.

```go
type CoordIJ struct {
    I, J int
}
```

<a name="CellToLocalIJ"></a>

### func [CellToLocalIJ](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1126>)

```go
func CellToLocalIJ(origin, cell Cell) (CoordIJ, error)
```

CellToLocalIJ produces ij coordinates for cell anchored by an origin.

The coordinate space used by this function may have deleted regions or warping due to pentagonal distortion.

Coordinates are only comparable if they come from the same origin index.

Failure may occur if the index is too far away from the origin or if the index is on the other side of a pentagon.

<a name="DirectedEdge"></a>

## type [DirectedEdge](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L145>)

DirectedEdge is an Index that identifies a directed edge between two cells.

```go
type DirectedEdge int64
```

<a name="DirectedEdgeFromString"></a>

### func [DirectedEdgeFromString](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L813>)

```go
func DirectedEdgeFromString(s string) DirectedEdge
```

DirectedEdgeFromString returns a DirectedEdge from a string. Should call e.IsValid\(\) to check if the DirectedEdge is valid before using it.

<a name="DirectedEdge.Boundary"></a>

### func \(DirectedEdge\) [Boundary](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L976>)

```go
func (e DirectedEdge) Boundary() (CellBoundary, error)
```

Boundary provides the coordinates of the boundary of the directed edge. Note, the type returned is CellBoundary, but the coordinates will be from the center of the origin to the center of the destination. There may be more than 2 coordinates to account for crossing faces.

<a name="DirectedEdge.Cells"></a>

### func \(DirectedEdge\) [Cells](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L963>)

```go
func (e DirectedEdge) Cells() ([]Cell, error)
```

Cells returns the origin and destination cells in that order.

<a name="DirectedEdge.Destination"></a>

### func \(DirectedEdge\) [Destination](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L955>)

```go
func (e DirectedEdge) Destination() (Cell, error)
```

Destination returns the destination cell of this directed edge.

<a name="DirectedEdge.IndexDigit"></a>

```go
func (e DirectedEdge) IndexDigit(resolution int) (int, error)
```

IndexDigit returns an [indexing digit](<https://h3geo.org/docs/library/index/cell>) of the edge.

```go
func (e DirectedEdge) IsValid() bool
```

IsValid determines if the directed edge is valid.



```go
func (e DirectedEdge) Resolution() int
```

Resolution returns the resolution of the edge.

<a name="DirectedEdge.String"></a>

```go
func (e DirectedEdge) String() string
```

<a name="GeoLoop"></a>

## type [GeoLoop](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L171>)

GeoLoop is a slice of LatLng points that make up a loop.

```go
type GeoLoop []LatLng
```

<a name="GeoPolygon"></a>

## type [GeoPolygon](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L179-L182>)

GeoPolygon is a GeoLoop with 0 or more GeoLoop holes.

```go
type GeoPolygon struct {
    GeoLoop GeoLoop
    Holes   []GeoLoop
}
```

<a name="CellsToMultiPolygon"></a>

### func [CellsToMultiPolygon](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L547>)

```go
func CellsToMultiPolygon(cells []Cell) ([]GeoPolygon, error)
```

CellsToMultiPolygon takes a set of cells and creates GeoPolygon\(s\) describing the outline\(s\) of a set of hexagons. Polygon outlines will follow GeoJSON MultiPolygon order: Each polygon will have one outer loop, which is first in the list, followed by any holes.

It is expected that all hexagons in the set have the same resolution and that the set contains no duplicates. Behavior is undefined if duplicates or multiple resolutions are present, and the algorithm may produce unexpected or invalid output.

<a name="GeoPolygon.Cells"></a>

### func \(GeoPolygon\) [Cells](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L535>)

```go
func (p GeoPolygon) Cells(resolution int) ([]Cell, error)
```

Cells takes a given GeoJSON\-like data structure fills it with the hexagon cells that are contained by the GeoJSON\-like data structure.

This implementation traces the GeoJSON geoloop\(s\) in cartesian space with hexagons, tests them and their neighbors to be contained by the geoloop\(s\), and then any newly found hexagons are used to test again until no new hexagons are found.

<a name="Index"></a>

## type [Index](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L155-L157>)

Index refers to an [H3 index](<https://h3geo.org/docs/core-library/h3Indexing>), which may be one of multiple modes to indicate the concept being indexed.

```go
type Index interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

## type [LatLng](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L174-L176>)

LatLng is a struct for geographic coordinates in degrees.

```go
type LatLng struct {
    Lat, Lng float64
}
```

```go
func CellToLatLng(c Cell) (LatLng, error)
```

CellToLatLng returns the geographic centerpoint of a Cell.

```go
func NewLatLng(lat, lng float64) LatLng
```

NewLatLng is a helper function to create a LatLng.

```go

```

VertexToLatLng returns the geographic coordinates of the vertex.


```go
func (g LatLng) String() string
```

<a name="Vertex"></a>

## type [Vertex](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L150>)

Vertex is an Index that identifies a single topological vertex, shared by three cells. A vertex is arbitrarily assigned one of the three neighboring cells as its "owner", which is used to calculate the canonical index and geographic coordinates for the vertex.

```go
type Vertex int64
```

<a name="CellToVertex"></a>

### func [CellToVertex](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1151>)

```go
func CellToVertex(c Cell, vertexNum int) (Vertex, error)
```

CellToVertex returns a single vertex for a given cell, or InvalidH3Index if the vertex is invalid.

<a name="CellToVertexes"></a>

### func [CellToVertexes](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1164>)

```go
func CellToVertexes(c Cell) ([]Vertex, error)
```

CellToVertexes returns all vertexes for the given cell.

<a name="VertexFromString"></a>

### func [VertexFromString](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L807>)

```go
func VertexFromString(s string) Vertex
```

VertexFromString returns a Vertex from a string. Should call v.IsValid\(\) to check if the Vertex is valid before using it.

<a name="Vertex.IndexDigit"></a>

### func \(Vertex\) [IndexDigit](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1222>)

```go
func (v Vertex) IndexDigit(resolution int) (int, error)
```

IndexDigit returns an [indexing digit](<https://h3geo.org/docs/library/index/cell>) of the vertex.

### func \(Vertex\) [IsValid](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1185>)

```go
func (v Vertex) IsValid() bool
```

IsValid returns whether the cell is a valid vertex.

<a name="Vertex.LatLng"></a>

### func \(Vertex\) [LatLng](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1173>)

```go
func (v Vertex) LatLng() (LatLng, error)
```

LatLng returns the geographic coordinates of the vertex.

<a name="Vertex.MarshalText"></a>

### func \(Vertex\) [MarshalText](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L1200>)

```go
func (v Vertex) MarshalText() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalText implements the encoding.TextMarshaler interface.

<a name="Vertex.Resolution"></a>

### func \(Vertex\) [Resolution](<https://github.com/uber/h3-go/blob/master/h3.go#L762>)

